patch.value:
## Charter
Reduce cognitive surface, not capability. The pm CLI keeps 100% of its features but presents them behind a small noun-verb grammar. This epic is the umbrella for the brainstormed consolidation clusters (each a child item) and the ADR that fixes the grammar rules.

## Grammar principles (brainstorm summary — ADR child formalizes)
1. Noun-first: one top-level command per domain noun (item lifecycle, list, history, package, schema, config, profile, plan, search, ops/diagnostics).
2. Verbs/facets are subcommands or flags, never new top-level commands.
3. Status/type projections are flags (--status, --type), not command variants.
4. Sugar commands (shortcuts, lifecycle aliases) are hidden aliases or package-provided, never core surface.
5. Nothing is deleted: every current spelling keeps working via an alias shim that prints a one-line migration hint (suppressible via config).
6. Contracts are the source of truth: help tiers, completions, MCP tools, and docs are all generated from the same contract table.

## Target surface (~15 nouns)
init, config, schema, profile, package (extension/install/upgrade), create (+meet/event/remind sugar), get, list (all status variants), update (update-many), close (close-many/delete/restore), claim (release/start/pause), item facets (comments/notes/learnings/files/docs/deps/test), history (redact/repair/compact/activity), search (eval), plan, context (next/focus), ops (stats/health/validate/gc/telemetry), contracts, help.

## Relationship to SDK-first epic
Consolidation is implemented on top of the SDK-first primitives (see linked epic under pm-ugqx): command shims become thin SDK calls, which is what makes the alias layer cheap and regression-safe.
CLI surface simplification: noun-verb consolidation to a minimal agent-first command grammar (zero feature loss)

Consolidate the ~60 top-level pm commands into a minimal, predictable noun-verb grammar so agents can operate the full surface from a small command set. Every existing feature and flag survives; removed spellings live on as hidden back-compat aliases. Duplicate check: searched simplify/consolidation/tiered-help/alias items — only closed historical polish (pm-5k2w, pm-rnpb) matched; no open overlap.

Top-level command list fits one help screen (~15 nouns); every pre-existing command spelling still works (hidden alias shims + deprecation notice); contracts/MCP/completions/docs regenerated with zero feature regression; agent token cost of 'pm --help' + contracts measurably reduced; all existing tests pass unchanged (aliases keep old spellings valid)
Shrink the top-level command surface (~60 -> ~15 nouns) without removing any feature or functionality
Agents discover and operate pm with less context; help/contract payloads shrink; fewer wrong-command retries

The command surface has grown command-by-command to ~60 top-level entries (8 list-* variants, 4 history commands, 4 package-lifecycle commands, 3 lifecycle aliases, 3 calendar shortcuts); agents report the CLI is getting too complicated

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Brainstorm census 2026-07-05 — current surface is ~60 top-level commands. Redundancy clusters found; 8 list-* status variants (list/all/draft/open/in-progress/blocked/closed/canceled), 5 history-family commands (history/history-redact/history-repair/history-compact/activity) plus restore, 4 package-lifecycle commands (extension/package/install/upgrade — extension and package are already partial synonyms), 3 lifecycle sugar commands (start-task/pause-task/close-task) duplicating claim/release/close+status, 3 calendar shortcuts (meet/event/remind) duplicating create with presets, 6 diagnostics commands (stats/health/validate/gc/telemetry/eval). Consolidating just these clusters removes ~24 top-level entries with zero feature loss.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Sequencing decision — alias shim layer (pm-0z7n) is the shared foundation and must land first after ADR pm-pbyu acceptance; each cluster task then becomes a mechanical table edit + spelling-parity spec. Token baseline (pm-a22j) runs before any change so the epic has measurable before/after evidence. Every rename must walk the full wiring checklist (commands/index, register-mutation, mcp/server, sdk/cli-contracts, contracts.ts, completions x3 shells, docs, vitest.config, contracts:update).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Surface growth pattern learned from history; features were added as new top-level commands because that was the path of least resistance in Commander registration — the grammar ADR plus contracts-generated help removes that incentive by making subcommand addition cheaper than top-level addition.

## Charter
Reduce cognitive surface, not capability. The pm CLI keeps 100% of its features but presents them behind a small noun-verb grammar. This epic is the umbrella for the brainstormed consolidation clusters (each a child item) and the ADR that fixes the grammar rules.

## Grammar principles (brainstorm summary — ADR child formalizes)
1. Noun-first: one top-level command per domain noun (item lifecycle, list, history, package, schema, config, profile, plan, search, ops/diagnostics).
2. Verbs/facets are subcommands or flags, never new top-level commands.
3. Status/type projections are flags (--status, --type), not command variants.
4. Sugar commands (shortcuts, lifecycle aliases) are hidden aliases or package-provided, never core surface.
5. Nothing is deleted: every current spelling keeps working via an alias shim that prints a one-line migration hint (suppressible via config).
6. Contracts are the source of truth: help tiers, completions, MCP tools, and docs are all generated from the same contract table.

## Target surface (~15 nouns)
init, config, schema, profile, package (extension/install/upgrade), create (+meet/event/remind sugar), get, list (all status variants), update (update-many), close (close-many/delete/restore), claim (release/start/pause), item facets (comments/notes/learnings/files/docs/deps/test), history (redact/repair/compact/activity), search (eval), plan, context (next/focus), ops (stats/health/validate/gc/telemetry), contracts, help.

## Relationship to SDK-first epic
Consolidation is implemented on top of the SDK-first primitives (see linked epic under pm-ugqx): command shims become thin SDK calls, which is what makes the alias layer cheap and regression-safe.

## Cluster index update (audit 2026-07-05, second pass)
Three target-surface clusters previously had no implementing child; filed: pm-yql1 (item annotation facets: comments/notes/learnings/files/docs/deps/append/test), pm-ik19 (bulk/destructive lifecycle: update-many/close-many/delete/restore), pm-kcs4 (navigation noun: context/next/focus). Mapping conflicts for restore (history vs close) and eval (ops vs search) recorded as a comment on ADR pm-pbyu for resolution at acceptance.

author: claude-code-agent

## Grammar principles (brainstorm summary — ADR child formalizes)
1. Noun-first: one top-level command per domain noun (item lifecycle, list, history, package, schema, config, profile, plan, search, ops/diagnostics).
2. Verbs/facets are subcommands or flags, never new top-level commands.

6. Contracts are the source of truth: help tiers, completions, MCP tools, and docs are all generated from the same contract table.

## Target surface (~15 nouns)
init, config, schema, profile, package (extension/install/upgrade), create (+meet/event/remind sugar), get, list (all status variants), update (update-many), close (close-many/delete), claim (release/start/pause), item facets (comments/notes/learnings/files/docs/deps/test), history (restore/redact/repair/compact/activity), search, plan, context (next/focus), ops (stats/health/validate/gc/telemetry/eval), contracts, help.

message: Resolve restore and eval ownership in CLI grammar charter

author: codex-sdk-agent

message: Align item facet target surface with append scope

value.author: codex-sdk-agent
value.text: Scope alignment evidence: PR #431 review caught that the target-surface body omitted append from item facets. Updated the live epic body through pm update so the consolidation grammar includes comments/notes/learnings/files/docs/deps/append/test.

author: codex-sdk-agent
message: Scope-alignment note complete for PR #431 review feedback

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: 2026-07-06 audit #7 (third pass): live top-level surface measured at 64 commands (pm --help). All previously-filed clusters still map 1:1 to children. Two unmapped spellings found and resolved by note/comment (no new items needed): 'aggregate' -> proposed as a pm list projection flag (query-read family, see ADR comment on pm-pbyu); 'test-all' -> ops verb, scope note added to pm-6apl. 'beads' and 'changelog' are package-owned commands and already conform to grammar rule 4 (sugar/package-provided, not core surface). New child added: pm-vxxm (GH-442 contracts --summary) — the contracts-side tier of the token-cost AC. Fresh dogfood evidence quantifying the epic's motivation now tracked under pm-mpbb: pm-cfed (JSON +48-53% vs TOON), pm-nilh (mutation echo 12.7x), pm-ishm (43% of context = repeated tags), pm-g9xk (constant error boilerplate).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Dogfood measurements (2026-07-05 external agent audit, GH-435/437/442/443/445) put hard numbers on the simplification thesis: bootstrap costs ~25KB (pm contracts) before the first real command; per-loop costs are dominated by output shape (full-item mutation echoes 12.7x over --id-only; tags 43% of pm context; JSON +48-53% over TOON). Consolidating the grammar (this epic) fixes discovery cost; the pm-mpbb lean-output cluster fixes loop cost. Both are needed to hit the agent token-cost AC.

value.author: codex
value.text: Audit evidence 2026-07-06/07 codex: live pm --help still exposes 64+ top-level/user-visible spellings plus extension commands and the internal test-runs-worker command. Existing child set covers list/history/package/lifecycle/calendar/facets/context/ops/aggregate/customization/help/contracts/alias-shim clusters. Duplicate-safe search found no focused item for the internal worker leakage, so pm-lp4j was created under this epic. This is a simplification gap only; no runtime feature is removed.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Planning evidence 2026-07-06/07 codex-agent: live contracts expose 101 command paths and 59 top-level spellings before consolidation. Existing child partition still covers the simplification strategy: ADR pm-pbyu, token baseline pm-a22j, alias shim pm-0z7n, list/history/package/lifecycle/calendar/facet/context/ops/aggregate/customization clusters, tiered help pm-e2bq, contracts/docs generation pm-mu8m, and internal-worker leakage pm-lp4j. Recommended planning stance: accept the ADR first, land alias shim second, then migrate one cluster at a time with spelling-parity tests and zero feature loss; do not remove any old spelling until hidden aliases and migration hints are test-gated.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: 2026-07-07 grammar re-verification + brainstorm additions. Live surface counted from pm --help: 65 top-level commands (63 core + 2 extension groups beads/changelog). Every core spelling maps to exactly one consolidation child (pm-pfqi list-*, pm-tqel history family, pm-tnud package family, pm-eq4x lifecycle sugar, pm-o3fh calendar sugar, pm-ik19 bulk/destructive, pm-kcs4 navigation, pm-yql1 annotation facets incl. test/test-all, pm-6apl ops, pm-xkgq aggregate, pm-npr3 customization family, pm-lp4j internal worker) — partition still complete, no new top-level command has leaked in since the last pass. New brainstorm points: (1) fix a SHARED VERB VOCABULARY across nouns so agents can guess subcommands (proposal commented on ADR pm-pbyu); (2) future query-power features must land as flags on existing nouns, never new nouns — filed pm-plwm (--where predicates) with that constraint stated in its AC; (3) tie the done-definition to the pm-a22j token baseline: target pm --help fitting one screen and contracts --summary (pm-vxxm) as the default agent bootstrap path.

5. Nothing is deleted: every current spelling keeps working via an alias shim that prints a one-line migration hint (suppressible via config).

author: claude-code-agent
message: Contracts summary contributes to noun-verb simplification bootstrap

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Bundle evidence: contracts --summary provides a cheap top-level command grammar bootstrap for progressive disclosure before agents request command-scoped flags.

value.note: Documents list recency shorthands and contracts summary retrieval primitive

patch.value: Adds token-minimal agent retrieval entries for the new primitives

value.note: Documents list recency shorthands and contracts summary retrieval primitive

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Verification evidence: full coverage passed at 100/100/100/100 (5462 tests), lint/static/docs/contracts gates passed, and packed-install temp proof passed in /tmp/pm-agent-ux-proof-TYSgf1 for pm list --today, pm list --recent, pm contracts --summary, and SDK runAction({action:'contracts', options:{summary:true}}).

author: claude-code-agent
message: Release claim after documenting current bundle progress; not closing unresolved scope.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Evidence update: CI Gates (static) failed because the committed contract snapshot was generated before rebuilding dist after tool-parameter metadata copy changes. Rebuilt with pnpm build, regenerated with pnpm contracts:update, and reran the full static CI sequence locally: pnpm version:check && pnpm changelog:pm:check && pnpm contracts:check && pnpm security:scan && pnpm lint && npm pack --dry-run. Result: passed.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Review evidence: addressed latest bot feedback before repush. Fixed SDK/MCP search contract leakage by keeping list-only --today/--recent out of search schemas, added help to contracts summary intents, treated programmatic updatedAfter:null as unselected, and clarified open-only today docs. Verification passed: focused tests (78 tests), CI-equivalent static sequence, and full coverage 100/100/100/100 (5463 tests).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Review evidence update: addressed the final Gemini normalizer finding by making normalizeExtensionFlagName accept unknown, ignore null/blank values, trim valid strings, and reject non-string values with a deterministic TypeError. Added focused coverage. Verification passed: focused tests (79 tests), CI-equivalent static sequence, and full coverage 100/100/100/100 (5464 tests).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Review evidence update: addressed final Gemini edge cases by ignoring blank updatedAfter values in list window conflict detection and adding more core command intents (guide, ops, reindex, focus, telemetry) to contracts summary output. Verification passed: focused tests (79 tests), CI-equivalent static sequence, and full coverage 100/100/100/100 (5464 tests).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Review evidence update: addressed CodeRabbit schema feedback by expanding the shared summary parameter description to include health rollups and adding contracts action schema mutual-exclusion rules for projection flags. Verification passed: focused tests (80 tests), CI-equivalent static sequence, and full coverage 100/100/100/100 (5465 tests).

value.note: README badge metadata touched during final verification bundle

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Review evidence update: addressed Greptile pushed-review schema/runtime mismatch by adding action-schema mutual-exclusion rules for list today/recent/updatedAfter windows across list variants. Verification passed: focused tests (81 tests), CI-equivalent static sequence, and full coverage 100/100/100/100 (5466 tests).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Review evidence update: addressed final value-sensitive schema feedback by making schema mutual exclusion conditional on selected boolean values, adding completion summary intent, and rejecting bare '-' short extension flags. Verification passed: focused tests (81 tests), CI-equivalent static sequence, and full coverage 100/100/100/100 (5466 tests).

author: codex-root-20260709
message: Session complete; release stale claim and leave unfinished scope open

author: codex-root-20260709
message: Session hygiene: no active agent is currently implementing this item; return unfinished scope to open

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Command-surface audit 2026-07-13: the live root help advertises 67 subcommands. They partition into history 6, list/query 9, item facets 8, package-owned governance/audit 6, claim lifecycle 4, close lifecycle 4, package lifecycle 4, context 3, calendar sugar 3, operations 7, stable remaining core 12, and one internal worker leak. Existing children already cover the consolidation work, so no duplicate simplification items were created. Proposed target remains a small noun-oriented grammar with status filters replacing list aliases, item facets grouped under item, lifecycle actions grouped consistently, package/extension commands grouped under package, and extension-owned audit commands discoverable without polluting the core surface. Preserve every capability, stable machine-readable contracts, shell composability, and compact default output.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Relationship organization 2026-07-13: agent story pm-1kxs now has blocked_by edges to all 15 active command-grammar delivery clusters required by its acceptance criteria: alias foundation, tiered help, list/history/package/lifecycle/calendar/bulk/navigation/facets/ops/aggregate/customization consolidation, internal-worker hiding, and contract-generated adapters/docs. Metrics-only pm-0mox remains non-blocking. This records the complete delivery graph without creating any duplicate simplification item.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Documented complementarity: n7rr consolidates the command surface; usfg supplies the primitives it shims over

author: maintainer-agent
message: The noun-verb grammar ADR governs every consolidation this epic executes

author: maintainer-agent
message: Token-surface program consumes the consolidated grammar; simplification wins compound as token wins

value.author: codex-audit-20260715
value.text: Review pass 91 agent-UX evidence: pm plan create executes as a positional action, but pm plan create --help --json returns unknown_command while pm plan --help advertises create and the help model exposes no subcommands. Focused issue pm-2tan was added under the command/help lineage to unify action help, contracts, and completion without broadening this umbrella.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Live grammar exemplar recorded 2026-07-18: `pm graph` (PR#580/#581) is the first large post-census command family shipped natively in the target noun-verb shape — ONE top-level noun with 12 verbs (neighbors/paths/closure/impact/subgraph/audit/profile/analyze/communities/redundancy/dominators/plan), subcommand enum single-sourced in sdk/cli-contracts/enum-contracts.ts, thin 7-line CLI shim (cli/graph.ts), one MCP tool. Net top-level surface cost of an entire graph platform: +1 command. This is the concrete before/after argument for the pbyu decision and the consolidation children (pfqi list-*, tqel history, tnud package, eq4x lifecycle): each cluster collapses N spellings to one noun exactly like graph. Also the counter-pressure evidence: every new first-class MCP tool costs ~+500 cold-start tokens (measurement on pm-9k90), so consolidation + profiles beat tool-count growth.
